projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: High-activity pattern: entryway averaging 21 camera observations/day over the last 7 days.

The sustained presence of 16-20 devices paired with zero camera activity for over three hours strongly suggests the household is gathered in a single, camera-blind zone (likely a home theater or basement) or the network is overcounting IoT devices. This clarifies why high network activity isn't triggering motion alerts and indicates a normal evening gathering rather than a system anomaly.

The consistent presence of 12-15 devices paired with zero camera motion since 16:00 indicates the household is likely stationary in a camera-blind area (e.g., garage, sunroom, or a room with cameras disabled) or the cameras are offline; given the Wednesday afternoon timing, this points to a relaxed family gathering or a potential system issue worth verifying.
High-activity pattern: entryway averaging 22 camera observations/day over the last 7 days.

High device presence (11-14) paired with zero camera motion for over three hours suggests the family is likely in a motion-blind zone or the cameras are offline/misconfigured; verify camera functionality or check if everyone is gathered in a single area.

A consistently high device count (12-14) paired with zero camera motion over a 4-hour Wednesday afternoon indicates a 'static occupancy' state, likely due to a school holiday, remote work/school day, or extended quiet period. This cross-signal pattern explains the lack of activity despite devices remaining connected and confirms the home is occupied but stationary.
High-activity pattern: front_yard averaging 35 camera observations/day over the last 7 days.

The gradual drop in connected mobile devices from ~21 to ~11 between 07:49 and 08:29 indicates a staggered morning departure for school and work, consistent with the household's typical Wednesday routine. This occupancy shift is normal and requires no action.

The sustained count of 17–18 connected devices paired with zero camera motion during late-night hours strongly indicates that Wi-Fi presence detection is registering IoT and network equipment rather than actual people, explaining the discrepancy with the four-person household and suggesting presence alerts should be tuned to filter non-mobile MAC addresses.

The combination of ~18 connected mobile devices and zero camera motion late on a Tuesday night strongly indicates a large, stationary group of guests or a house party rather than normal family activity. Steve should verify the gathering is authorized and monitor for noise or safety concerns.
